description_long: This sculpture depicts a crowned female figure, likely the Virgin Mary, holding a child in her left arm, both rendered in a classical, draped style. The figures stand atop a rugged, naturalistic stone base that gives the impression of being carved from a single block. The woman's robe flows gracefully, with detailed folds and a serene facial expression, while the child is nestled securely against her. The crown atop the woman's head is ornate, adding a regal quality to the piece. The stone base is irregular and textured, contrasting with the smoother, more refined surfaces of the figures. The overall coloration is a pale, weathered stone, suggesting the use of marble or limestone.
description: A pale stone sculpture of a crowned woman holding a child, standing atop a rugged, naturalistic stone base. The figures are finely detailed, with flowing robes and serene expressions.
description_view_0: Front view showing the crowned woman holding the child, both faces and details of the robes clearly visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of both figures and the depth of the drapery, with the stone base's texture prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the woman's robe and the full extent of the stone base, with minimal detail on the figures' faces.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the child in profile and the flowing lines of the woman's robe, as well as the rugged stone base.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation environment.
